text.title: Vector Database Selection
text.summary: Provider comparison, index type selection rules, and critical pitfalls for vector database setup.

list.item: Pinecone: managed SaaS, serverless, auto-scale, pay-per-use — best for teams wanting zero infra ops
Qdrant: open-source, self-hosted Docker/K8s, best performance + filtering + quantization at scale
Chroma: open-source, in-process SQLite, zero infra — best for local dev and eval harnesses
Weaviate: open-source/managed, schema-first, GraphQL API, built-in vectorizer integrations
Milvus: open-source, K8s-native, enterprise scale (billions of vectors)
pgvector: PostgreSQL extension — best when the application already uses Postgres and vector count is moderate

rule.statement: Use HNSW for real-time search requiring <10ms latency and datasets up to tens of millions of vectors.
rule.rationale: HNSW achieves 95-99% recall with the lowest query latency of any ANN index. Memory overhead is ~40-50 bytes per vector per HNSW link.

rule.statement: Use IVF for memory-constrained environments where query latency of 10-50ms is acceptable.
rule.rationale: IVF divides the space into Voronoi cells; query probes only a subset of cells, trading some recall for lower memory usage than HNSW.

rule.statement: Use Flat (exact brute-force) only for datasets with fewer than 10K vectors.
rule.rationale: Flat search is O(n); at 10K vectors it remains under 10ms, but at 1M vectors it becomes prohibitively slow.

rule.statement: Choose the distance metric (cosine, L2, dot product) before creating the collection and treat it as immutable; changing it requires recreating the collection and re-ingesting all data.
rule.rationale: Vector DB indexes are built for a specific metric. Using the wrong metric produces incorrect similarity rankings; correcting it is a full re-index.

rule.statement: Validate embedding dimensions match the collection definition before ingesting; dimension mismatches cause silent failures in some clients.
rule.rationale: Some clients silently truncate or reject mismatched vectors. Catching this at ingestion time prevents a corrupt index.

antipattern.description: Storing full document text as metadata (kilobytes per record).
antipattern.reason: Metadata is loaded into memory at query time. Large payloads bloat RAM and slow query throughput. Store text externally; keep only IDs and small filter fields in metadata.

antipattern.description: Using a cloud managed DB (Pinecone Serverless) for hard real-time SLAs without testing cold-start latency.
antipattern.reason: Serverless instances have cold-start latency on the first query after idle periods. Not suitable for sub-100ms P99 requirements.

antipattern.description: Not implementing a document update/delete strategy before bulk ingestion.
antipattern.reason: Vector DBs have no referential integrity. Deleted source documents leave stale chunks in the index. Plan delete+re-insert or soft-delete tracking before the first ingest.
